title: orthilia secunda subsp. obtusata
intro: Orthilia secunda subsp. obtusata, also known as a type of mountain cranberry or bearberry, is a low-growing, evergreen shrub native to the mountainous regions of Europe. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include astringent and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Orthilia secunda subsp. obtusata include its ability to support urinary tract health, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant activity. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
